catalog.title: Reading · Everyday genres in the wild
catalog.description: Everyday genres in the wild · A2 reading in “Transport & city”: Dutch you can use the same week, guided steps, and a short check at the end.

pedagogy.objective: By the end of this lesson, you can read an authentic-style text about transport & city and extract both gist and specific information
can_do_outcomes: Read departure boards and simple travel apps with support
Handle a simple ticket or gate problem with help phrases
Extract gist and one supporting detail from a short text.

grammar_points.point: motion verbs + preposition (naar, met, van … naar …)
examples_nl: We reizen van Utrecht naar Amsterdam.
Ik ga met de bus naar het station.
examples_en: We're travelling from Utrecht to Amsterdam.
I'm taking the bus to the station.

examples_nl: Ik ga vanavond uit eten met een vriend.
We gaan morgen naar het museum.
examples_en: I'm going out for dinner with a friend tonight.
We're going to the museum tomorrow.

grammar_points.point: future with gaan + infinitive for plans
examples_nl: Wanneer je incheckt, vergeet niet uit te checken.
Als de trein vertraging heeft, stuur ik een appje.
examples_en: When you check in, don't forget to check out.
If the train is delayed, I'll send a text.

micro_outcomes: I can recognise key words about transport & city in short Dutch texts or audio.
I can copy at least one useful chunk into a sentence about myself.
I can finish most guided tasks in the lesson without peeking at translations on the second try.
I can pass the quick self-check at the end of the lesson.
I know what to revise tomorrow in two minutes or less.

steps.activity:
Welcome! This lesson is built in small steps so you always know where you are.

**What you'll be able to do:** Read an authentic-style text about transport & city and extract both gist and specific information

**Grammar thread today:** Motion + prepositions (naar, met, van … naar …) — you'll get explanations, examples, and production tasks in **Language focus** and **Guided practice**.

Take your time — you can repeat any step. Try to say at least one Dutch sentence out loud today.
steps.teacher_notes: Keep under ~1 min; reassure anxious learners.

steps.activity:
Let's wake up vocabulary about **Transport & city**.

Words you might use today:
• **kaartje**
• **dienstregeling**
• **metro**
• **OV-chipkaart**
• **inchecken**
• **uitchecken**
• **vertraging**
• **spoor**

**Your turn (1–2 short sentences, Dutch or mixed):**
**Wat deed je gisteren?** — *What did you do yesterday?*

If you only know a few words, that's fine — start there.
steps.example_response: Gisteren nam ik de bus naar het centrum en wisselde ik één keer over. Ik was een beetje te laat, maar ik stuurde een appje: *Sorry, ik ben zo daar.*

illustration.alt: Simple route from A to B with a transfer stop in the middle and OV label

steps.activity:
**Reading focus**

Read this real-life style text slowly.

[E-mail van sportvereniging]
Beste lid,
Training op dinsdag vervalt vanwege schoolactiviteiten in de hal. We trainen donderdag op hetzelfde tijdstip.
Groet,
Het bestuur

**After reading:** in one English sentence, what is this text mainly about?

**Today's grammar — motion + prepositions**

**Rule:** *naar* (towards), *met* (by/with), *van … naar …* for routes.

**Patterns:**
• *Ik ga met de bus naar het centrum.* · *We fietsen van huis naar het station.*

**Produce:**
1. Describe one real trip with *van … naar …*.
2. Add *met de trein / met de auto*.

**Pronunciation (listen + repeat)**
**Pronunciation — v / f:** *vandaag, vier* — light *v*, not English *w*.

**Language focus — patterns you can reuse**

**motion verbs + preposition (naar, met, van … naar …)**
• NL: We reizen van Utrecht naar Amsterdam. / Ik ga met de bus naar het station.
• EN: We're travelling from Utrecht to Amsterdam. / I'm taking the bus to the station.

**connectors: eerst, daarna, totdat (simple sequences)**
• NL: Ik ga vanavond uit eten met een vriend. / We gaan morgen naar het museum.
• EN: I'm going out for dinner with a friend tonight. / We're going to the museum tomorrow.

**future with gaan + infinitive for plans**
• NL: Wanneer je incheckt, vergeet niet uit te checken. / Als de trein vertraging heeft, stuur ik een appje.
• EN: When you check in, don't forget to check out. / If the train is delayed, I'll send a text.

**Try this:** pick one Dutch sentence above and change only the time or the person (ik → wij / vandaag → morgen), **or** do the “Produce” task from the grammar box above.

**1 — Headline:** give this thread a title in English (max 6 words).

**2 — Times:** list every time or date you see.

**3 — Who must do what?** One sentence in English.

**4 — Gap-fill from text:** "De lift is morgen … voor onderhoud." *(from context)*

**5 — Problem / solution:** what is the problem and what is suggested?

**6 — Word guess:** what might **onderhoud** mean?

**7 — True / false:** "Everyone is angry in this chat."

**8 — Your reply:** write one short Dutch sentence you could post in this group.

**Text:**
[E-mail van sportvereniging]
Beste lid,
Training op dinsdag vervalt vanwege schoolactiviteiten in de hal. We trainen donderdag op hetzelfde tijdstip.
Groet,
Het bestuur
---

**All four skills — finish this lesson with these four micro-tasks**

🎧 **Listening:** Use TTS on this Dutch line (slow, then closer to natural): *[E-mail van sportvereniging]*

📖 **Reading:** Read silently, then say the **main idea in one English sentence**: *[E-mail van sportvereniging]*

✍️ **Writing:** Write **one new Dutch sentence** that uses **kaartje** and fits *Motion + prepositions (naar, met, van … naar …)*.

🗣️ **Speaking:** Read your new sentence **aloud twice** — exaggerate stress the first time, normal the second.

*(If this lesson was already heavy on one skill, treat that skill as “extra reps” — the point is you always touch all four.)*
steps.teacher_notes: Mix modalities; at least 6 tasks — all concrete above.

steps.activity:
**Phrase bank (use freely)**
• kaartje
• dienstregeling
• metro
• OV-chipkaart
• inchecken
• uitchecken
• vertraging
• spoor
• perron
•  overstap
• fietsenstalling
• route

**Your task:** Write or say **4–6 short sentences** in Dutch about this unit theme.
Rules: include **at least one question** and one connector (**en**, **maar**, or **want**).

**Example starter (you can change it):**
Vandaag … Ik wil … **want** … **En jij?**

**Quick self-check**

Try each question, then tap **Check answer**. The correct answer stays hidden until you submit.

**Tip:** Say Dutch options out loud if that helps.

**Afterwards:** If three or more felt easy, open **Quiz** for a short recap. If not, go back to **Guided practice** once more.
steps.teacher_notes: Rendered with interactive UI when interaction is present.

items.prompt: Pick the best reply when someone says **Hoe gaat het?**
items.feedbackCorrect: Nice — this is a natural, friendly reply.
items.feedbackIncorrect: **Tot ziens** is goodbye; **Graag gedaan** fits after *dank je*. After *Hoe gaat het?* people often say how they feel, e.g. **Goed, dank je!**

items.hint: Present tense of *leren* (to learn), 1st person singular.

items.prompt: Which opening sounds **more formal** when you talk to a stranger or in a shop?
items.feedbackCorrect: Right — **u** + verb form (*Kunt u…*) is the usual polite pattern.
items.feedbackIncorrect: **Kunt u** with *u* is more formal; **Kun je** is fine with friends.

items.prompt: In Dutch, yes/no questions often put the **verb before the subject** (e.g. *Ga je mee?*).
items.feedbackCorrect: Correct — verb-first order is typical for yes/no questions.
items.feedbackIncorrect: This is **true**: many yes/no questions start with the verb.

items.prompt: Say or write **one new sentence** using **kaartje**.
items.exampleAnswer: Example: *Ik wil graag kaartje met mijn nieuwe buren.* (adjust to your situation.)

steps.activity:
**Nice work.**

Next in this unit: **Speaking · Say it out loud—then shorten it** — we keep building **Transport & city** with a new focus.

**2-minute revision:** skim the phrase bank from step 6 once more.

When you're ready, open **Quiz** for a compact recap — or **Flashcards** for the lemmas.

**Dutch culture insight**

In cities, **parking and blue zones** (*betaald parkeren*) confuse almost everyone once. Reading the small sign (*ma–za 09:00–21:00*) saves fines.

**Phrases — OV & delays**

| Dutch | English |
|-------|--------|
| *Excuseer, welke tram gaat naar …?* | Excuse me, which tram goes to …? |
| *Ik moet overstappen bij …* | I have to transfer at … |
| *Er is vertraging door werkzaamheden.* | There is a delay due to engineering works. |

**Mini dialogue**

**Reiziger:** Hoe laat vertrekt de volgende bus naar het ziekenhuis?
**Medewerker:** Over zes minuten, halte twee. Vergeet niet in te checken.

**Say or write:** Say you are late and will send an app using *Sorry, ik heb vertraging*.

*Optional reflection:* In one English sentence, what feels similar or different where you live?
steps.teacher_notes: Link to next lesson id in CMS when wired.

quiz_ideas: Self-check: choose the best reply to "Hoe gaat het?" (see step 7 in the lesson).
Gap-fill idea: write a sentence with **kaartje** in the present tense.
True/false: in Dutch yes/no questions the verb often comes before the subject.
Say one new polite request starting with **Kunt u …** or **Mag ik …**.
Paraphrase one Dutch line from the lesson input in your own English.
assessment.success_criteria: You can complete the step-7 self-check comfortably and produce at least one clear Dutch sentence about the lesson theme (spoken or written).
